---
title: "FW 536 — Day 1 afternoon lab (v2 · marine & fisheries) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 1 afternoon lab — Discrete distributions problem set

**Problem set version:** **v2 — marine & fisheries theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — terrestrial wildlife & disease](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 6 — Bycatch species composition

> In a shrimp trawl in the Gulf of Mexico, the species composition of bycatch is shrimp 0.55, croaker 0.20, drum 0.15, other 0.10. A single tow yields 200 fish.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — Designing a trawl survey for over-dispersed counts

> Pre-survey power analysis: catches per tow have historical mean = 25 fish and variance = 200 fish. You're choosing between fitting a Poisson and a NB model.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Tag-recapture

> You release 100 marked salmon smolts and recapture 8 of them out of a sample of 50. (Hypergeometric approximated by binomial when population is large.)

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — Microplastic particles in mussels

> Counts of microplastic particles per mussel follow Poisson($\lambda$). $\lambda$ varies across sites. At site A, $\lambda = 2$; at site B, $\lambda = 8$.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Voter on the dock

> 20 commercial fishers gather to vote on a regulation. Each will vote "yes" independently with probability 0.4. The motion passes if more than half vote yes.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
